steps.title: Keep preposition and object together
steps.content: The sentence needs the preposition on to connect directly to its object, US currency. Punctuation between a preposition and its object would break that connection.

steps.title: Use no punctuation
steps.content: A separates the preposition from its object with a semicolon, B with a comma, and C with a colon. The sentence is grammatical only when on flows straight into US currency. The answer is D.
